body:
## Problem Statement

The diagnostics system has a complete configuration schema but it is not documented anywhere. Users cannot discover or tune diagnostics settings without reading source code.

## Current State

Configuration exists in `adapteros-config` but is undocumented:
- `diagnostics.enabled` - Enable/disable
- `diagnostics.level` - Verbosity level
- `diagnostics.channel_capacity` - Buffer size
- `diagnostics.batch_size` - Persistence batch size
- `diagnostics.batch_timeout_ms` - Flush timeout
- `diagnostics.max_events_per_run` - Per-run limit

## Impact

- Users cannot tune diagnostics for their workload
- Default values may not be appropriate for all deployments
- Performance implications of settings are unknown

## Acceptance Criteria

### Configuration Reference (Required)

- [ ] Add to `configs/README.md` or create `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`
- [ ] Document all diagnostics settings with:
  - [ ] Type and default value
  - [ ] Valid ranges
  - [ ] Performance implications
  - [ ] Example use cases

### CLAUDE.md Update (Required)

- [ ] Add minimal config example in CLI section
- [ ] Link to full reference

## Suggested Documentation

```markdown
## Diagnostics Configuration

```toml
[diagnostics]
# Enable diagnostic event capture
enabled = true

# Verbosity level: "off", "errors", "stages", "router", "tokens"
# - off: No events captured (zero overhead)
# - errors: Only failures and errors
# - stages: Stage enter/exit events (recommended for production)
# - router: + Routing decisions (useful for adapter debugging)
# - tokens: + Token-level detail (high volume, dev only)
level = "stages"

# Event buffer capacity before backpressure
# Higher = more memory, fewer drops under load
# Default: 1000
channel_capacity = 1000

# Events per SQLite transaction
# Higher = better throughput, higher latency
# Default: 100
batch_size = 100

# Max milliseconds before forcing flush
# Lower = fresher data, more I/O
# Default: 500
batch_timeout_ms = 500

# Maximum events per inference run
# Prevents runaway runs from exhausting storage
# Default: 10000
max_events_per_run = 10000
```

### Performance Tuning Guide

| Scenario | Recommended Settings |
|----------|---------------------|
| Production (minimal overhead) | `level = "errors"`, `batch_size = 200` |
| Production (auditable) | `level = "stages"`, `batch_size = 100` |
| Debugging routing | `level = "router"` |
| Deep debugging | `level = "tokens"`, `max_events_per_run = 50000` |
| High-throughput | `channel_capacity = 5000`, `batch_size = 500` |
```

## Priority

**P3** - Documentation improvement.

## Labels

`documentation`, `configuration`, `diagnostics`, `P3`
title: docs(config): Add diagnostics configuration schema reference

body:
## Problem Statement

Developers have no runnable examples showing how to use the diagnostics infrastructure. The only way to learn is reading 2,766 lines of source code or reverse-engineering test files.

## Current State

```
crates/adapteros-diagnostics/
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs          # 1,063 lines
│   ├── service.rs      # 596 lines
│   ├── writer.rs       # 560 lines
│   ├── stage_guard.rs  # 320 lines
│   └── run_tracker.rs  # 227 lines
├── Cargo.toml
└── (NO examples/ directory)
```

## Proposed Structure

```
crates/adapteros-diagnostics/
├── examples/
│   ├── basic_emission.rs       # Minimal "hello world"
│   ├── stage_guard_pipeline.rs # Multi-stage inference simulation
│   ├── custom_event_types.rs   # Application-specific events
│   ├── query_persisted.rs      # Read back from SQLite
│   └── backpressure_demo.rs    # High-volume emission patterns
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### basic_emission.rs (Required)
- [ ] 30 lines or less
- [ ] Demonstrates: create service, emit event, verify receipt
- [ ] No external dependencies beyond crate
- [ ] Runnable with `cargo run --example basic_emission`

### stage_guard_pipeline.rs (Required)
- [ ] Simulates 5-stage inference pipeline
- [ ] Uses StageGuard RAII pattern correctly
- [ ] Shows timing capture
- [ ] Demonstrates error handling (fail vs complete)

### custom_event_types.rs (Required)
- [ ] Shows how to emit `DiagEvent::Custom` variants
- [ ] Demonstrates payload structure
- [ ] Shows canonical hashing for custom data

### query_persisted.rs (Required)
- [ ] Sets up in-memory SQLite
- [ ] Emits, persists, then queries events
- [ ] Shows filtering by run_id, stage, severity

### backpressure_demo.rs (Optional)
- [ ] Demonstrates high-volume emission
- [ ] Shows metrics for dropped events
- [ ] Explains tuning parameters

## Example: basic_emission.rs

```rust
//! Minimal example: emit a diagnostic event
//!
//! Run with: cargo run --example basic_emission

use adapteros_diagnostics::{
    DiagnosticsService, DiagnosticsConfig, DiagEvent, DiagRunId, DiagStage,
};

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    // Create service with default config
    let config = DiagnosticsConfig::default();
    let (service, mut receiver) = DiagnosticsService::new(config);

    // Emit a run started event
    let run_id = DiagRunId::new();
    service.emit(DiagEvent::RunStarted {
        run_id: run_id.clone(),
        is_replay: false,
        tenant_id: "example-tenant".into(),
    });

    // Receive the event
    if let Some(envelope) = receiver.recv().await {
        println!("Received event: {:?}", envelope.event);
        println!("Timestamp: {} µs", envelope.mono_us);
        println!("Hash: {:?}", envelope.event_hash);
    }
}
```

## Priority

**P3** - Developer experience improvement.

## Labels

`documentation`, `diagnostics`, `developer-experience`, `examples`, `P3`
title: feat(cli): Add diagnostic examples directory with runnable samples

body:
## Problem Statement

While the diagnostics module has excellent unit test coverage (47 tests), there are no end-to-end integration tests that verify the complete diagnostic flow from event emission to persistence to retrieval.

## Current Test Coverage

| Component | Unit Tests | Integration Tests |
|-----------|------------|-------------------|
| Event schema | ✅ 22 tests | ❌ None |
| DiagnosticsService | ✅ 10 tests | ❌ None |
| DiagnosticsWriter | ✅ 5 tests | ❌ None |
| RunTracker | ✅ 6 tests | ❌ None |
| StageGuard | ✅ 4 tests | ❌ None |
| **End-to-end flow** | N/A | ❌ None |

## Missing Test Scenarios

### 1. Full Pipeline Test
- Emit event via DiagnosticsService
- Writer batches and persists to SQLite
- Query back via SqliteDiagPersister
- Verify event integrity (hash, sequence, content)

### 2. Inference Integration Test
- Start inference with diagnostics enabled
- Verify RunStarted event emitted
- Verify stage events captured with correct timing
- Verify RunFinished/RunFailed on completion

### 3. Bundle Export/Verify Roundtrip
- Generate diagnostic bundle from test run
- Export bundle via CLI
- Verify bundle via CLI (offline mode)
- Confirm all events present with correct hashes

### 4. Backpressure Behavior Test
- Emit events faster than writer can persist
- Verify graceful degradation (drops, not crashes)
- Verify metrics track dropped events
- Verify no data corruption on remaining events

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add `tests/diagnostics_e2e_tests.rs` with:
  - [ ] `test_emit_persist_retrieve_roundtrip`
  - [ ] `test_inference_pipeline_captures_all_stages`
  - [ ] `test_bundle_export_verify_roundtrip`
  - [ ] `test_backpressure_graceful_degradation`
- [ ] Tests must run without external dependencies (in-memory SQLite)
- [ ] Tests must be deterministic (no flaky timing issues)
- [ ] Add to CI pipeline

## Technical Notes

Test setup pattern:
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_emit_persist_retrieve_roundtrip() {
    // Setup in-memory SQLite
    let pool = setup_test_db().await;
    let persister = SqliteDiagPersister::new_arc(pool.clone());
    
    // Create service and writer
    let config = DiagnosticsConfig::default();
    let (service, receiver) = DiagnosticsService::new(config);
    let writer = DiagnosticsWriter::new(persister.clone(), WriterConfig::default(), Arc::new(RunTracker::new()));
    
    // Emit events
    let run_id = DiagRunId::new();
    service.emit(DiagEvent::RunStarted { ... }).await;
    
    // Trigger flush
    drop(service);
    writer.shutdown().await;
    
    // Query and verify
    let events = persister.get_events_for_run(&run_id).await?;
    assert_eq!(events.len(), 1);
    assert!(matches!(events[0].event, DiagEvent::RunStarted { .. }));
}
```

## Priority

**P2** - Improves confidence in production reliability.

## Labels

`testing`, `diagnostics`, `integration-tests`, `P2`
title: fix(diagnostics): Add missing integration tests for end-to-end diagnostic flow

body:
## Problem Statement

The `aosctl diag export` and `aosctl diag run` commands have capabilities that are not documented, preventing users from leveraging the full power of the diagnostic system.

## Undocumented Features

### 1. `--full-db` Flag
- **What it does**: Exports complete database state instead of summary
- **Location**: `crates/adapteros-cli/src/commands/diag_bundle.rs`
- **Current docs**: Not mentioned in CLAUDE.md or `--help`

### 2. CPID-Specific Telemetry Filtering
- **What it does**: Filter diagnostic bundles by specific CPID (conversation/process ID)
- **Use case**: Debugging specific inference runs without full bundle
- **Current docs**: Not documented

### 3. Verbosity Levels Detail
- **What it does**: 5 levels control diagnostic capture granularity
- **Levels**: Off, Errors, Stages, Router, Tokens
- **Current docs**: Mentioned but not explained

### 4. Bundle Verification Offline Mode
- **What it does**: Verify bundle integrity without server connection
- **Use case**: Air-gapped audit verification
- **Current docs**: Not documented

## Acceptance Criteria

### CLAUDE.md Updates (Required)

- [ ] Document `--full-db` flag with use cases
- [ ] Document CPID filtering capability
- [ ] Add verbosity level table with descriptions
- [ ] Document offline verification workflow

### CLI Help Text Updates (Required)

- [ ] Add examples to `aosctl diag export --help`
- [ ] Add examples to `aosctl diag run --help`
- [ ] Include common workflows in help text

## Suggested Documentation

```markdown
## Diagnostic Bundle Export

### Basic Export
\`\`\`bash
aosctl diag export --trace-id <id> --output ./bundle.tar.zst
\`\`\`

### Full Database Export (for deep debugging)
\`\`\`bash
aosctl diag export --trace-id <id> --full-db --output ./bundle.tar.zst
\`\`\`

### CPID-Specific Export
\`\`\`bash
aosctl diag export --cpid <cpid> --output ./bundle.tar.zst
\`\`\`

### Offline Verification
\`\`\`bash
aosctl diag verify ./bundle.tar.zst  # No server required
\`\`\`

## Diagnostic Verbosity Levels

| Level | Captured Events | Use Case |
|-------|-----------------|----------|
| `off` | None | Production (minimal overhead) |
| `errors` | Failures only | Production monitoring |
| `stages` | Stage enter/exit | Performance profiling |
| `router` | + Routing decisions | Adapter selection debugging |
| `tokens` | + Token-level detail | Deep inference debugging |

Set in config:
\`\`\`toml
[diagnostics]
level = "router"
\`\`\`
```

## Priority

**P3** - Enhancement to existing documentation, not blocking.

## Labels

`documentation`, `cli`, `diagnostics`, `enhancement`, `P3`
title: docs(cli): Document undocumented diag bundle flags and capabilities

body:
## Problem Statement

Several CLI commands are documented or implied to exist but are actually stubbed or unimplemented. Users attempting these commands receive cryptic errors without understanding the feature is unavailable.

## Affected Commands

| Command | Status | Current Behavior |
|---------|--------|------------------|
| `aosctl aos migrate` | Stubbed | Shows generic error |
| `aosctl aos convert` | Stubbed | Shows generic error |
| `aosctl node sync --air-gap` | Not implemented | Shows "not yet implemented" |
| `aosctl node sync export` | Not implemented | Shows "not yet implemented" |
| `aosctl cdp list` | Awaiting integration | Partial functionality |
| `aosctl migrate adapter` | Not implemented | Shows error |
| `aosctl serve --backend coreml` | Feature-gated | Shows "CoreML backend not yet implemented" |

## Impact

- **User frustration**: Commands appear in help but don't work
- **Wasted time**: Users attempt workflows that cannot succeed
- **Documentation distrust**: If some documented features don't work, users question all docs

## Acceptance Criteria

### CLAUDE.md Updates (Required)

- [ ] Add "Feature Status" section listing all commands with their status
- [ ] Mark unimplemented commands with ⚠️ or similar indicator
- [ ] Provide workarounds where applicable

### CLI Help Text Updates (Required)

- [ ] Add `[COMING SOON]` or `[NOT IMPLEMENTED]` suffix to stubbed commands in `--help` output
- [ ] Example: `aosctl aos migrate [NOT IMPLEMENTED]   Migrate adapter format`

### Error Message Improvements (Required)

- [ ] Stubbed commands should show: `"This command is not yet implemented. Track progress at: <issue-link>"`
- [ ] Include expected availability if known

## Suggested CLAUDE.md Section

```markdown
## Command Status

| Command | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `aosctl doctor` | ✅ Stable | |
| `aosctl diag run` | ✅ Stable | |
| `aosctl diag export` | ✅ Stable | |
| `aosctl serve` | ✅ Stable | MLX backend only |
| `aosctl serve --backend coreml` | ⚠️ Not implemented | CoreML backend pending |
| `aosctl aos migrate` | ⚠️ Not implemented | Adapter format migration |
| `aosctl node sync --air-gap` | ⚠️ Not implemented | Air-gapped deployment |
```

## Technical Notes

Locations requiring updates:
- `crates/adapteros-cli/src/commands/aos.rs:22` - aos migrate/convert stubs
- `crates/adapteros-cli/src/commands/node_sync.rs` - air-gap sync
- `crates/adapteros-cli/src/commands/serve.rs:354-357` - CoreML gate
- `CLAUDE.md` - CLI section

## Priority

**P2** - Reduces user confusion but no breaking functionality.

## Labels

`documentation`, `cli`, `developer-experience`, `P2`
title: docs(cli): Document stubbed/unimplemented commands in CLAUDE.md

body:
## Problem Statement

The `adapteros-diagnostics` crate has excellent internal API documentation but **zero external documentation**, making it impossible for new developers to understand how to integrate diagnostics into their code.

## Current State

| Documentation Type | Status |
|-------------------|--------|
| API doc comments | ✅ Excellent (all public types documented) |
| README.md | ❌ Missing |
| examples/ directory | ❌ Missing |
| CLAUDE.md coverage | ⚠️ 1 line only |
| Integration guide | ❌ Missing |
| Configuration reference | ❌ Missing |

## Impact

- **Onboarding friction**: New developers cannot use diagnostics without reading 2,766 lines of source
- **Feature discoverability**: Users don't know diagnostics exists or how to enable it
- **Support burden**: Questions that documentation should answer become support tickets

## Acceptance Criteria

### README.md (Required)

- [ ] Create `crates/adapteros-diagnostics/README.md` with:
  - [ ] Purpose and overview (what problems it solves)
  - [ ] Quick start example (10 lines to emit first event)
  - [ ] Configuration reference table
  - [ ] Link to detailed docs

### Examples Directory (Required)

- [ ] Create `crates/adapteros-diagnostics/examples/` with:
  - [ ] `basic_emission.rs` - Minimal event emission
  - [ ] `stage_guard_usage.rs` - RAII pattern for pipeline stages
  - [ ] `custom_events.rs` - Emitting application-specific events

### CLAUDE.md Section (Required)

- [ ] Add "Diagnostics Infrastructure" section to CLAUDE.md with:
  - [ ] Configuration snippet showing how to enable
  - [ ] Verbosity levels table (Off, Errors, Stages, Router, Tokens)
  - [ ] Example StageGuard usage
  - [ ] Environment variables (`AOS_DEBUG_DETERMINISM`)

### Integration Guide (Required)

- [ ] Create `docs/DIAGNOSTICS_GUIDE.md` with:
  - [ ] Architecture overview (event schema, levels, stages)
  - [ ] Step-by-step integration checklist
  - [ ] Query examples (how to retrieve stored events)
  - [ ] Troubleshooting matrix

## Technical Context

The diagnostics module is production-ready with:
- 47 passing tests
- Full SQLite persistence via `SqliteDiagPersister`
- 23 event types covering 11 pipeline stages
- Non-blocking emission with backpressure handling

The code quality is high; only documentation is missing.

## Suggested README.md Structure

```markdown
# adapteros-diagnostics

Deterministic diagnostic event capture for AdapterOS inference pipelines.

## Quick Start

\`\`\`rust
use adapteros_diagnostics::{DiagnosticsService, DiagnosticsConfig, StageGuard, DiagStage};

// Initialize service
let config = DiagnosticsConfig::default();
let (service, receiver) = DiagnosticsService::new(config);

// Emit events using StageGuard (RAII)
let guard = StageGuard::new(&service, DiagStage::RequestValidation, &trace_ctx, &run_id, "tenant");
// ... do work ...
guard.complete(); // or guard.fail("E1001")
\`\`\`

## Configuration

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `enabled` | bool | true | Enable/disable diagnostics |
| `level` | string | "stages" | Verbosity: off, errors, stages, router, tokens |
| `channel_capacity` | usize | 1000 | Event buffer size |
| `batch_size` | usize | 100 | Events per persistence batch |
| `batch_timeout_ms` | u64 | 500 | Max wait before flush |

## Event Types

See `DiagEvent` enum for all 23 event types covering the full inference pipeline.
```

## Priority

**P2** - Documentation gap blocks adoption but doesn't break existing functionality.

## Labels

`documentation`, `diagnostics`, `developer-experience`, `P2`
title: docs(diagnostics): Add README.md and integration guide for diagnostics crate

body:
## Problem Statement

The `aosctl review` command displays a misleading error message that causes user confusion and erodes trust in the system.

**Current behavior**: When the server is unavailable, the CLI shows:
```
Review endpoints not yet implemented on server
```

**Actual state**: The review endpoints ARE fully implemented in `adapteros-server-api/src/handlers/review.rs`:
- `GET /v1/infer/{id}/state`
- `POST /v1/infer/{id}/review`
- `GET /v1/infer/paused`
- `GET /v1/infer/{id}/pause-details`
- `POST /v1/reviews/submit`

## Impact

- **User confusion**: Users believe the feature doesn't exist when it does
- **Support burden**: Users may file bug reports for "missing" functionality
- **Trust erosion**: Undermines confidence in documentation accuracy

## Location

`crates/adapteros-cli/src/commands/review.rs:285`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Error message updated to indicate server connectivity issue, not missing implementation
- [ ] Message should read: `"Failed to connect to server at {url}. Ensure the server is running."`
- [ ] Include actionable guidance (e.g., "Run 'aosctl serve' to start the server")
- [ ] Unit test added to verify correct error message on connection failure

## Technical Notes

The current error path:
```rust
// Line 285 - INCORRECT MESSAGE
output.warn("Review endpoints not yet implemented on server");
```

Should become:
```rust
output.error(&format!("Failed to connect to server at {}. Ensure the server is running.", server_url));
output.hint("Start the server with: aosctl serve --config configs/cp.toml");
```

## Priority

**P1** - Misleading error messages are breaking issues that affect user trust.

## Labels

`bug`, `cli`, `documentation`, `P1`
title: fix(cli): Misleading error message in review command claims endpoints not implemented
PRD: Standardize partial_cmp Error Handling Across Codebase
PRD: Apply Zeroize Pattern to KMS Credentials

body:
# PRD: Implement Remaining 28 Boot Invariants

## 1. Overview

### Problem Statement
Boot invariant analysis identified 28 additional invariants beyond the ~10 currently implemented. Missing invariants allow the server to boot with potentially invalid state.

### Objective
Implement the documented invariants to ensure system starts in a validated state, with appropriate fail-open vs fail-closed behavior.

## 2. Current State

### Implemented Invariants (~10)
Located in `crates/adapteros-server/src/boot/invariants.rs`:
- Token signing key presence
- Database connection health
- Migrations applied
- Production security flags
- Model path existence

### Documented But Not Implemented (28)
From `invariants.rs:420-470`:

**Security (9 remaining):**
- SEC-006: JWT signature verification
- SEC-007: Tenant isolation
- SEC-008: RBAC permission checks
- SEC-009: Token revocation baseline
- SEC-010: Hardware attestation
- SEC-011: Quorum signature verification
- SEC-012: Adapter bundle signature
- SEC-013: Password timing-safety
- SEC-014: Brute force protection

**Data Integrity (7):**
- DAT-001: Archive state machine
- DAT-002: Foreign key constraints
- DAT-003: AOS file hash match
- DAT-004: KV presence for readiness
- DAT-005: Enum constraints
- DAT-006: Migration ordering
- DAT-007: Audit log chain (FAILS OPEN)

**Memory Management (4):**
- MEM-001: KV cache generation coherence
- MEM-002: GPU buffer fingerprint
- MEM-003: Memory pressure headroom (FAILS OPEN)
- MEM-004: KV slab non-overlapping (FAILS OPEN)

**Concurrency (4):**
- CON-001: Hot-swap atomic pointer (FAILS OPEN)
- CON-002: KV quota transactional
- CON-003: Model cache pinning (FAILS OPEN)
- CON-004: Request pin refcount

**Lifecycle (4):**
- LIF-001: Boot phase ordering (FAILS OPEN)
- LIF-002: Global executor init
- LIF-003: Adapter lifecycle CAS
- LIF-004: (implicit in lifecycle module)

## 3. Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| INV-001 | Implement all P0 security invariants (SEC-*) | P0 |
| INV-002 | Implement data integrity invariants (DAT-*) | P0 |
| INV-003 | Mark FAILS OPEN invariants with explicit log | P1 |
| INV-004 | Add metrics for invariant check latency | P1 |
| INV-005 | Document each invariant's failure mode | P0 |

## 4. Technical Design

### 4.1 Invariant Structure
```rust
pub struct Invariant {
    pub id: &'static str,           // "SEC-006"
    pub name: &'static str,         // "JWT signature verification"
    pub category: InvariantCategory,
    pub failure_mode: FailureMode,  // FailOpen or FailClosed
    pub check: Box<dyn Fn(&BootContext) -> InvariantResult>,
}

pub enum FailureMode {
    FailOpen { log_level: Level },   // Log warning, continue
    FailClosed,                       // Fatal error
}
```

### 4.2 Example Invariant Implementation
```rust
// SEC-012: Adapter bundle signature
fn check_adapter_signatures(ctx: &BootContext) -> InvariantResult {
    if !ctx.config.production_mode {
        return InvariantResult::Skipped("dev mode");
    }
    
    for adapter in ctx.db.list_adapters().await? {
        if adapter.signature.is_none() {
            return InvariantResult::Violation {
                id: "SEC-012",
                message: format!("Adapter {} has no signature", adapter.id),
                is_fatal: true,
            };
        }
    }
    InvariantResult::Pass
}
```

### 4.3 Boot Phase Integration
```rust
pub async fn run_boot_invariants(ctx: &BootContext) -> Result<InvariantReport> {
    let mut report = InvariantReport::new();
    
    // Phase 1: Security invariants (fail-closed)
    for inv in SECURITY_INVARIANTS {
        report.check(inv, ctx).await?;
    }
    
    // Phase 2: Data integrity (fail-closed)
    for inv in DATA_INVARIANTS {
        report.check(inv, ctx).await?;
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Memory/Concurrency (fail-open with logging)
    for inv in RUNTIME_INVARIANTS {
        if let Err(e) = report.check(inv, ctx).await {
            warn!("Invariant {} failed (continuing): {}", inv.id, e);
        }
    }
    
    Ok(report)
}
```

## 5. Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Security Invariants (Week 1)
- SEC-006, SEC-007, SEC-008 (auth-related)
- SEC-011, SEC-012 (signature-related)

### Phase 2: Data Integrity (Week 2)
- DAT-001 through DAT-007
- Add database constraint validation

### Phase 3: Runtime Invariants (Week 3)
- MEM-*, CON-*, LIF-*
- Mark fail-open appropriately

## 6. Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `server/src/boot/invariants.rs` | Add 28 invariant checks |
| `server/src/boot/mod.rs` | Wire into boot sequence |
| `core/src/errors.rs` | Add InvariantViolation error type |

## 7. Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 28 invariants have check functions
- [ ] Fail-open invariants log at WARN level
- [ ] Fail-closed invariants abort boot in production
- [ ] `aosctl doctor` reports invariant status
- [ ] Boot time increase <500ms with all checks

## 8. Reference

See plan file: `~/.claude/plans/vivid-imagining-rocket.md`
title: PRD: Implement Remaining 28 Boot Invariants
PRD: Complete KV Backend Migration Testing

body:
# PRD: Implement Heap Observer Allocator Hooks

## 1. Overview

### Problem Statement
The Metal heap observer uses hardcoded 15% estimates for internal fragmentation instead of tracking actual free-block distribution.

### Objective
Implement allocator hooks to track actual heap fragmentation.

## 2. Current State

### Hardcoded Estimates (heap_observer.rs:576-580)
```rust
// TODO: Replace this hardcoded estimate with actual free-block tracking
let estimated_waste = total_allocated as f32 * 0.15;  // Hardcoded 15%
```

### Existing Infrastructure
- FFI-safe structures for C/ObjC interop
- Metal heap integration via `metal::Heap`
- `MTLHeap.maxAvailableSizeWithAlignment` for largest contiguous block

## 3. Requirements

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| HEP-001 | Track actual free-block count and sizes | P0 |
| HEP-002 | Calculate real fragmentation ratio | P0 |
| HEP-003 | Expose metrics via FFI | P1 |

## 4. Technical Design

### Free Block Tracking
```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct FreeBlockStats {
    pub block_count: u32,
    pub total_free_bytes: u64,
    pub largest_block_bytes: u64,
}

impl FreeBlockStats {
    pub fn fragmentation_ratio(&self) -> f32 {
        if self.block_count <= 1 { return 0.0; }
        1.0 - (self.largest_block_bytes as f32 / self.total_free_bytes as f32)
    }
}
```

### Metal Heap Query
```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
fn query_heap_fragmentation(heap: &metal::Heap) -> FreeBlockStats {
    let max_available = heap.max_available_size_with_alignment(16);
    let total_free = heap.size().saturating_sub(heap.current_allocated_size());
    FreeBlockStats {
        block_count: ((total_free / max_available.max(1)) as u32).max(1),
        total_free_bytes: total_free,
        largest_block_bytes: max_available,
    }
}
```
